title: Barba-CV Schema
description: Open-source deterministic JSON schema for structured CV data. Barba-CV v1.2 keeps the CV payload at root for backward compatibility while introducing a meta block and an extensions area.

barba_cv_version.description: Version of the Barba-CV payload format. Version 1 corresponded to raw Barba-CV JSON without formal schema metadata. Version 1.2 introduces this field, a meta block, and an extensions area.

profile_summary.description: Free-text professional summary or profile statement. May be empty or omitted.

position_sought.description: Target roles or positioning statements describing the candidate's desired position or professional orientation. Often used as the headline of the CV.

experiences.description: Professional experience entries. May be empty or omitted.

education.description: Education entries. May be empty or omitted.

certifications.description: Certification entries. May be empty or omitted.

languages.description: Languages known by the candidate. Language level remains flexible and human-readable.

interests.description: Interests, hobbies, or other personal activities.

project_achievements.description: Structured project or achievement extracts derived from the CV. Replaces the legacy projects_achievements_extracts key.

cv_uuid.description: Unique identifier for the CV within processing pipelines or databases. Typically a UUID generated by the CV processing engine.

cv_title.description: Human-readable title for the CV used for export, UI display, or filename generation.

parsing_errors.description: Parser or mapping issues detected during extraction. In v1 this lived at root; in v1.2 it moves under meta.

ats_processed.description: Whether the JSON content reflects ATS-compatible post-processing rather than raw extraction only.

processor_engine.description: Engine or processor identifier that generated or revised the payload, e.g. barba_cv_engine_1.1.

parsed_at.description: Human-readable parse timestamp or ingestion timestamp. Left flexible to avoid rejecting operational data.

source_original_text.description: Original extracted text source when stored alongside the payload.

source_ats_revised_text.description: ATS-oriented revised text representation when available.

source_format.description: Original source format such as pdf, docx, html, or text.

ingested_at.description: Human-readable timestamp for database ingestion or downstream storage.

embedded_at.description: Human-readable timestamp indicating when vector embedding or downstream enrichment occurred.

extraction_confidence_overall.description: Optional overall confidence score for AI extraction, between 0 and 1.
